content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Bind validated resolution to connection behavior or an egress proxy and verify re-resolution and mixed-answer cases.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Dns Rebinding Boundary model · Theory Dns Rebind…, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Dns Rebinding Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Dns Rebinding Boundary model · Theory Dns Rebind…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 9388 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to block unsafe schemes, addresses, redirects, rebinding, proxies, and metadata while retaining causal evidence; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bind validated resolution to connection behavior or an egress proxy and verify re-resolution and mixed-answer cases.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Dns Rebinding Boundary model · Theory Dns Rebind…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Checking DNS once before fetch defeats rebinding.
